Feature importance for uplift modeling (filter methods)

Source: R/filters.R
get_importance.Rd

Ranks features by strength of treatment-by-feature interaction or by bin-based divergence. For binary outcome (classification) only.

Usage

get_importance(
  data,
  features,
  y_name,
  method,
  experiment_group_column = "treatment_group_key",
  control_group = "control",
  treatment_group = "treatment",
  n_bins = 5,
  null_impute = NULL,
  order = 1,
  disp = FALSE
)

Arguments

data

data.frame containing outcome, features, and experiment group column

features

character vector of feature column names

y_name

column name of the outcome variable (binary)

method

one of "F", "LR", "KL", "ED", "Chi"

experiment_group_column

column name for treatment assignment (e.g. "treatment_group_key")

control_group

value in that column for control (e.g. "control")

treatment_group

value in that column for treatment (e.g. "treatment1")

n_bins

number of bins for KL/ED/Chi (default 5)

null_impute

for KL/ED/Chi: "mean", "median", or "most_frequent"; if NULL and NA present, error

order

for F and LR: 1=linear, 2=quadratic+linear, 3=cubic+quadratic+linear (default 1)

disp

unused; kept for API compatibility

Value

data.frame with columns method, feature, rank, score, p_value, misc

Details

Methods: - F: F-test of treatment-feature interaction (linear/quadratic/cubic). - LR: Likelihood-ratio test (logistic regression) of interaction. - KL, ED, Chi: Bin-based divergence (KL, Euclidean, Chi-Square).

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# get_importance(...)
} # }
